subject: Update oparent version to 1.1.0
commit_message:
Update oparent version to 1.1.0

Directive from ONAP project that all repos should be updated.
Change vierified with a build with .m2 /org/onap cleared.

The test cases are updated becasue changing the oparent version changed the
order of test execution and tests failed due to the static data in PAPServices
not being cleared. Clearing of PAPServices data in those test cases is now
done in an @After method.

The test case in the test below has a test dependency:
ONAP-PDP-REST/src/test/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/rest/notifications/test/NotificationTest.java
depends on com.att.aft.dme2 version 3.1.200-oss

That dependency was added to the POM of ONAP-PDP-REST

Issue-ID: POLICY-693
Change-Id: Ie070f9e94216f4a81f114380782a834ce8663fd5
Signed-off-by: liamfallon <liam.fallon@ericsson.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

Yes, thinking about it afterwards, I did wonder why test results were being notified to somewhere with a notification service. I didn't really understand what the test was testing.

Patch Set 1:

Ravi - can you help us understand why we need to add com.att.aft.dme2 in order to run the use case? My understanding was that dependency was not to be used in ONAP.

Patch Set 1:

Pam, I don't think the dependency added by Liam in PDP-REST pom is not required.

But let me confirm on updating the oparent. we use dme2 jars for AFF.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

Hi Ravindra, It is required. When you run the test:
ONAP-PDP-REST/src/test/java/org/onap/policy/pdp/rest/notifications/test/NotificationTest.java

deep deep down in the call chain, the notification service loads this class using reflection. Because it's loaded by reflection, it doesn't show up in the static dependencies.

Patch Set 1:

Hi again Ravindra, of course it's only required because that test is there. I don't understand why that test is there or what it's testing so if we remove the test case then the problem goes away!

Patch Set 1:

I am very leery of this, I'm thinking we @Ignore this unit test and remove the dependency. Probably we should keep the fixes that clear PAPServices data since ordering can change.

Patch Set 1:

OK. I'll do that and submit a new review in a minute.

I'm sure PAPServices data clearing is needed and is unrelated to the NotificationService thing.

Actually its funny because the other tests in that class also call the notification service but they don't seem to trigger loading of that class. Pam maybe open a Jira to have a look at this later.

Patch Set 1:

Apologies!!!

Another twist on this story, I just now realise I had the incorrect test class referenced in the check in message. The class that causes that dependency is actually:

org.onap.policy.pdp.rest.api.test.NotificationAPITest

Test case that fails is the last one in that class:
threadTest()

The exception is:

Patch Set 1:

So actually in PolicyEngineUtils, that pom does include dme:
		<dependency>
			<groupId>com.att.aft</groupId>
			<artifactId>dme2</artifactId>
			<version>3.1.200</version>
		</dependency>

Patch Set 1:

So perhaps now I feel a bit better including it - although I think other AT&T projects are not using dme2. I will investigate post-Beijing.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully merged by Pamela Dragosh
